text.title: Copywriting Formulas, Headline Writing, and Power Words
text.summary: Four core copywriting formulas (AIDA/PAS/BAB/4Ps), headline formula library, power word categories, and readability targets.

rule.statement: Conduct Voice of Customer research before writing any copy; document exact phrases customers use in reviews, support tickets, and Reddit before starting.
rule.rationale: VoC language mirrors the audience's own words back to them, eliminating the "this isn't for me" filter. Copy written without VoC data defaults to internal product language that audiences cannot relate to.

list.item: Customer interviews: direct quotes about pain points and desired outcomes
Support tickets: exact language of frustrations and blockers
Reviews (yours + competitors): phrases like "I wish...", "My biggest frustration is...", "What I really need is..."
Reddit and forums: unfiltered audience language in niche communities

rule.statement: Choose one formula per copy piece; mixing formulas in the same piece produces confusing structure.
rule.rationale: Each formula creates a specific emotional journey. Mixing them breaks the reader's logical flow and reduces conversion.

list.item: AIDA: Attention (headline) → Interest (relevance hook) → Desire (benefits) → Action (CTA) — classic, works for most contexts
PAS: Problem (state the pain) → Agitate (amplify emotional impact) → Solution (introduce product) — best for high-pain ICP
4Ps: Promise (what you'll deliver) → Picture (paint the future state) → Proof (why to believe) → Push (drive to action)
BAB: Before (current painful state) → After (desired future state) → Bridge (your product bridges the gap) — best for transformation stories

rule.statement: Write 10 headline variants minimum before selecting one; the first headline is almost never the best.
rule.rationale: Headlines determine 80% of copy effectiveness. The first version is a warm-up; versions 6-10 typically produce 2-3x more specific and compelling options.

list.item: How to [Benefit]: "How to Write Copy That Converts"
[Number] Ways to [Result]: "7 Ways to Double Your Signups"
The Secret to [Outcome]: "The Secret to Landing Page Success"
[Question]?: "Struggling to Get Customers?"
Why [Surprising Claim]: "Why Your Best Copy Gets Ignored"
[Result] Without [Pain]: "Get Customers Without Cold Calling"
[Result] in [Timeframe]: "Double Revenue in 90 Days"
Stop [Problem]: "Stop Losing Visitors at Checkout"
Urgency: Now, today, immediately, limited, expires
Exclusivity: Secret, insider, members-only, private
Trust: Proven, guaranteed, tested, verified
Ease: Simple, quick, easy, effortless, in minutes
Results: Transform, unlock, discover, achieve
Emotion: Imagine, feel, experience, picture yourself

rule.statement: Ban generic power words ("revolutionary," "game-changing," "cutting-edge") explicitly in agent prompts.
rule.rationale: LLMs trained on marketing copy default to these clichés. Explicit banning produces more specific and credible language.

list.item: Reading level: 6th-8th grade (Flesch-Kincaid)
Sentence length: under 20 words maximum
Paragraph length: under 4 sentences
"You" vs "We" ratio: 2:1 in favor of "you"
Active voice: 90%+ of sentences
